commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human approval is the remaining action because the patch changes a security/compatibility boundary under a protected maintainer label; there is no narrow automated repair needed.; Cleared: The diff tightens host-exec environment sanitization and changes only policy/test/generated mirror files, with no new dependency, workflow, secret, or code-execution supply-chain concern found. (sha=514c182dc53e15e22dd46a876755b7f32edb1deb)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `RUSTUP_HOME` and `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN` to the host-env override blocklist, preserves inherited rustup values, updates reported baseline coverage and tests, and regenerates the macOS policy mirror.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +21, Generated +2. Total +29 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main lacks `RUSTUP_*` in the override blocklist, and the sanitizer rejects only policy-listed override keys; PR head adds those keys and shows after-fix sanitizer output.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human approval is the remaining action because the patch changes a security/compatibility boundary under a protected maintainer label; there is no narrow automated repair needed.

target.title: fix: block rustup toolchain env overrides [AI]
target.body:
## Summary
- Block rustup toolchain relocation variables in host exec environment overrides.
- Preserve trusted inherited rustup settings with explicit rationale and regression coverage.
- Extend reported host-env baseline coverage for the new blocked keys and regenerate the macOS policy mirror.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: agent/request supplied `RUSTUP_HOME` / `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN` no longer survive host exec environment sanitization, while trusted inherited operator rustup settings remain available.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node through the source sanitizer path, plus the repo Vitest wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node --import tsx -e 'import { sanitizeHostExecEnvWithDiagnostics } from "./src/infra/host-env-security.ts"; ...'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:host-env-policy:swift`

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "rejectedOverrideBlockedKeys": [
    "RUSTUP_HOME",
    "RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN"
  ],
  "inheritedRustupHome": "/trusted/rustup",
  "inheritedRustupToolchain": "stable",
  "overrideRustupHomeSurvived": false,
  "overrideRustupToolchainSurvived": false
}
```

Observed result after fix: live sanitizer proof rejected both request overrides, preserved trusted inherited rustup values, and confirmed neither override value survived. Focused Vitest also passed 3 files / 34 tests, and the generated Swift policy check reported `OK apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/HostEnvSecurityPolicy.generated.swift`.

What was not tested: no live rustup toolchain execution was run; the sanitizer boundary is covered directly at the policy and host-env merge point.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx -e 'import { sanitizeHostExecEnvWithDiagnostics } from "./src/infra/host-env-security.ts"; ...'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:host-env-policy:swift`

AI-assisted: yes
target.author: pgondhi987

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
